landlord-safety-certificate.body: Our engineers inspect fixed wiring, consumer units, earthing and bonding, then grade observations so you know what is urgently required. faq: How often do landlords need an electrical safety certificate?

In England, private landlords must ensure electrical installations are inspected and tested at least every five years, or at each new tenancy if the existing report has expired, unless a shorter interval is stated on the report.
Is a landlord safety certificate the same as an EICR?
Yes in practice — the Electrical Installation Condition Report is the standard document used to evidence electrical safety for rented homes.
Can you certificate multiple rental properties?
We regularly schedule portfolio days for agents and landlords with several addresses across Greater Manchester and nearby counties.

faq: Is fire alarm servicing a legal requirement?
Fire safety law requires equipment to be maintained in efficient working order. BS 5839 servicing is the recognised way to evidence that duty.
What is the difference between servicing and a weekly test?
Weekly tests are usually done by site staff (e.g. a call point). Servicing is a deeper competent-person inspection and test regime.
Will servicing stop false alarms?
It reduces many causes — dirty detectors, weak batteries and wiring issues — though some false alarms need design or environmental changes.

faq: What is the difference between detection and a full fire alarm system?
Detection refers to the sensors; a full system also includes control panels, manual call points, sounders/beacons and power supplies. We deliver complete packages.
Which detector type suits a kitchen?
Heat or multi-sensor devices with appropriate algorithms are often preferred over standard smoke detectors to reduce cooking-related false alarms.
Can detection be added to an existing panel?
If the panel has spare capacity and compatible protocols, yes. Otherwise we quote a panel upgrade with new detection loops.

faq: Do rental properties need interlinked smoke alarms?
Requirements depend on property type and location, but interlinked alarms on each storey are widely expected. We advise based on your risk assessment and landlord duties.
Should smoke alarms be on a full fire alarm panel?
Houses may use stand-alone or radio-interlinked alarms; larger blocks and commercial sites often need a BS 5839 system. We recommend the right approach on survey.
How often should smoke alarms be replaced?
Manufacturers typically specify around ten years for sealed units. We check manufacture dates during visits and replace expired devices.

faq: Can heat detectors replace all smoke detectors?
No. Heat detectors generally respond more slowly to smouldering fires and are used selectively where smoke detection is unsuitable.
Are heat detectors suitable for landlord kitchens?
Yes — heat detection is commonly used in kitchen areas within larger fire alarm or multi-sensor schemes.
Do you retrofit heat detectors onto existing systems?
We can add compatible devices if the panel and cabling support them, after checking loop loading and zoning.

fire-alarm-certification.intro: Fire alarm certification documents design, installation, commissioning or servicing to recognised BS 5839 expectations. Icomply issues the certificates dutyholders need after works across Stockport and the wider North West.
fire-alarm-certification.body: Whether you require a new system design certificate, installation and commissioning pack, or periodic service records, paperwork is completed only after the technical work checks out. Certificates link to zone information and cause-and-effect so auditors see a coherent story. Digital and paper copies can be supplied for multi-site portfolios.
fire-alarm-certification.meta_desc: Fire alarm certification & BS 5839 paperwork for install, commission & service. Stockport & North West coverage by Icomply.
focus_points: Design, installation, commissioning and service certificates
Cause-and-effect and zone documentation aligned to works
Logbook updates for the responsible person
Portfolio-friendly records for multi-site clients
faq: When do I receive fire alarm certificates?
After successful commissioning or service, once any critical defects affecting certification have been resolved.
Do insurers ask for fire alarm certificates?
Many commercial policies expect evidence of maintenance and, for new systems, commissioning certification. We provide industry-recognised documents.
Can you certificate a system installed by others?
We can service and maintain third-party systems and issue service records. Full installation certification only covers work we have verified or completed.
fire-alarm-certification.seo_keywords: fire alarm certification, BS 5839 certificate Stockport, fire alarm commissioning certificate North West, fire system certificate Manchester, Icomply

fire-alarm-compliance.intro: Fire alarm compliance means your system matches the building’s risk profile and is maintained so it actually works in an emergency. Icomply helps North West dutyholders close gaps flagged by fire risk assessments and enforcing officers.
fire-alarm-compliance.body: We translate FRA actions into practical upgrades — extra detection, better sounder levels, disabled refuge links or modern panels — with costed options. Maintenance contracts then lock in the ongoing evidence trail. Our Stockport team works with landlords, agents and commercial occupiers who need straightforward compliance, not jargon.
fire-alarm-compliance.meta_desc: Fire alarm compliance support across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. FRA actions, BS 5839 upgrades & maintenance by Icomply.
focus_points: FRA action plans turned into engineered fire alarm solutions
Category and coverage upgrades for changed building use
Maintenance programmes that evidence ongoing compliance
Liaison-ready documentation for audits and inspections
faq: What if my fire risk assessment says the alarm is inadequate?
We survey the existing system, propose a compliant design upgrade and implement agreed works with full certification.
Does a working panel automatically mean compliance?
Not always. Coverage category, device types, sounder levels and maintenance records all matter alongside a powered panel.
Can you help after a Fire Service visit?
Yes. We prioritise enforcement-related defects and provide clear evidence of corrective action.
fire-alarm-compliance.seo_keywords: fire alarm compliance, fire alarm FRA actions Stockport, BS 5839 compliance North West, fire safety compliance Manchester, landlord fire alarm compliance, Icomply

bs-5839.intro: BS 5839 is the key British Standard series guiding design, installation, commissioning and maintenance of fire detection and fire alarm systems in non-domestic premises. Icomply applies BS 5839 practice on every relevant project from our Stockport base.
bs-5839.body: Clients often hear BS 5839 mentioned in risk assessments without a clear explanation of categories, grades or servicing intervals. We map the right parts of the standard to your building, then deliver works and paperwork that stand up to scrutiny. Training notes help responsible persons complete their weekly duties correctly.
bs-5839.meta_desc: BS 5839 fire alarm design, install, service & certification across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. Expert support from Icomply.
focus_points: Design categories explained in practical building language
Installation and commissioning aligned with BS 5839
Maintenance intervals and competent-person servicing
Documentation packs auditors recognise and accept
faq: What does BS 5839 cover?
It sets recommendations for fire detection and alarm systems in non-domestic buildings, including design categories, installation quality, commissioning and maintenance.
Is BS 5839 a legal requirement?
The law requires suitable fire safety measures; BS 5839 is the primary benchmark used to demonstrate that fire alarms meet that duty.
Which BS 5839 category do I need?
Categories such as L1–L5, M and P depend on risk and building use. We recommend a category after survey and review of your fire risk assessment.
bs-5839.seo_keywords: BS 5839, BS 5839 fire alarm, BS 5839 servicing Stockport, BS 5839 installation North West, fire alarm standard Manchester, Icomply BS 5839

addressable-fire-alarm-panel.intro: An addressable fire alarm panel pinpoints the exact device in alarm or fault, saving vital minutes in larger buildings. Icomply supplies, programmes and commissions addressable panels for North West commercial and multi-residential sites.
addressable-fire-alarm-panel.body: We select panels with the loop capacity, networking and cause-and-effect features your estate needs — without overselling unused modules. Programming includes clear device labels, not cryptic codes, so staff can respond confidently. Migration paths from conventional systems are planned to limit downtime.
addressable-fire-alarm-panel.meta_desc: Addressable fire alarm panels supplied, programmed & commissioned across Stockport & the North West. BS 5839 installs by Icomply.
focus_points: Panel selection for loop size, networking and future expansion
Device addressing with plain-English location text
Cause-and-effect programming for doors, plant and interfaces
Commissioning, user training and certificate packs
faq: When should I choose an addressable panel over conventional?
Larger or multi-zone buildings, sites with frequent false alarms, or premises needing precise device location usually benefit from addressable technology.
Can you replace our panel and keep existing detectors?
Sometimes, if detectors use a compatible protocol. Often a phased device upgrade is safer — we advise after identifying head types.
Do you work with major addressable brands?
Yes. We regularly install and service popular UK commercial panels and associated field devices.
addressable-fire-alarm-panel.seo_keywords: addressable fire alarm panel, addressable panel install Stockport, addressable fire system North West, fire panel programming Manchester, Icomply addressable

conventional-fire-alarm-panel.intro: A conventional fire alarm panel remains a cost-effective, robust choice for smaller premises with straightforward zoning. Icomply designs and installs conventional systems for shops, offices and small blocks across the North West.
conventional-fire-alarm-panel.body: Zones are laid out so responding staff know which area needs attention, with sounder circuits and power supplies sized correctly. We avoid stretching conventional kits beyond their practical limits — recommending addressable upgrades when buildings grow complex. Handover includes zone charts that match the real floor plan.
conventional-fire-alarm-panel.meta_desc: Conventional fire alarm panels installed & serviced in Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. Clear zoning & BS 5839 from Icomply.
focus_points: Right-sized conventional panels for smaller buildings
Logical zone design and wall-mounted zone charts
Sounder circuit and battery autonomy calculations
Service, repair and upgrade paths as sites expand
faq: Are conventional fire alarms still compliant?
Yes, when correctly designed and maintained to BS 5839 for the building’s risk. Compliance depends on design quality, not only technology type.
How many zones do I need?
Enough to identify the fire location quickly without unnecessary cost. We set zone boundaries using floor plans and occupancy patterns.
Can a conventional panel monitor plant or lifts?
Ancillary relays can signal plant shutdowns and other interfaces; we include them in cause-and-effect design where required.
conventional-fire-alarm-panel.seo_keywords: conventional fire alarm panel, conventional fire alarm Stockport, zone fire panel North West, small commercial fire alarm Manchester, Icomply conventional

wireless-fire-alarm-system.intro: A wireless fire alarm system reduces cable runs in listed buildings, live hotels and fast-track refurbishments. Icomply surveys radio performance and installs wireless or hybrid BS 5839 solutions across the North West.
wireless-fire-alarm-system.body: We never treat wireless as a shortcut past proper design — device placement, signal surveys and battery maintenance plans are essential. Hybrid systems can blend wired backbones with wireless field devices where that is the smartest mix. Clients receive guidance on battery change cycles so protection does not quietly expire.
wireless-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: Wireless fire alarm systems for difficult sites across Stockport, Manchester & the North West. Survey-led BS 5839 installs by Icomply.
focus_points: Radio survey before committing to wireless devices
Full wireless or hybrid wired/wireless architectures
Battery lifecycle planning and maintenance schedules
Commissioning equal in rigour to wired systems
faq: Are wireless fire alarms acceptable under BS 5839?
Yes, when products are suitable and the system is designed, installed and maintained correctly, including radio performance and power supplies.
How often do wireless device batteries need changing?
Intervals vary by manufacturer and device type, often measured in years. We log expected dates and replace batteries during service visits.
Is wireless cheaper than a wired system?
It can reduce labour in hard-to-cable buildings, though equipment cost may be higher. We compare whole-life cost on each survey.
wireless-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: wireless fire alarm system, wireless fire alarm Stockport, radio fire alarm North West, hybrid fire alarm Manchester, BS 5839 wireless, Icomply

fire-alarm-upgrade.intro: A fire alarm upgrade modernises obsolete panels, improves detection coverage and cuts false alarms without always starting from scratch. Icomply plans phased upgrades for North West estates that cannot afford long shutdowns.
fire-alarm-upgrade.body: We audit what can be retained — sounder circuits, containment or selected devices — and what must go. Phased programmes keep parts of the building protected throughout the works. Capital quotes show options from minimum compliance to future-ready addressable platforms.
fire-alarm-upgrade.meta_desc: Fire alarm upgrades & panel modernisation across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. Phased BS 5839 projects by Icomply.
focus_points: Condition audit of existing panels, devices and cabling
Phased upgrade plans that maintain protection during works
False-alarm reduction through better device selection
Full recommissioning and updated compliance documents
faq: When is an upgrade better than endless repairs?
When spares are obsolete, false alarms are frequent, or the system category no longer matches building use, upgrades usually cost less long term.
Can we upgrade floor by floor?
Often yes with careful isolation and temporary measures. We programme phases around occupancy and trading needs.
Will staff need retraining after an upgrade?
Yes — new panel interfaces and procedures are covered in our handover so weekly tests stay effective.
fire-alarm-upgrade.seo_keywords: fire alarm upgrade, fire panel upgrade Stockport, modernise fire alarm North West, replace obsolete fire alarm Manchester, BS 5839 upgrade, Icomply

fire-safety-certificate.intro: A fire safety certificate in the property compliance sense usually means documented evidence that fire alarm or related life-safety works meet the required standard. Icomply provides the certificates and service records North West clients need after fire alarm projects.
fire-safety-certificate.body: We clarify what document you actually need — commissioning certificates, service reports or supporting evidence for a wider fire safety file — because terminology varies between landlords and agents. Our packs are structured for audits rather than buried in generic invoices. Stockport engineers complete technical checks before any certificate is signed.
fire-safety-certificate.meta_desc: Fire safety certificates & fire alarm compliance paperwork across Stockport, Manchester & the North West. Clear BS 5839 docs from Icomply.
focus_points: Commissioning and service certificates for fire alarm works
Evidence packs aligned with landlord and agent requests
Logbook entries that support the wider fire safety file
Plain-English explanation of what each document covers
faq: Is there a single national ‘fire safety certificate’ for buildings?
Terminology varies. For fire alarms, the key documents are design, installation, commissioning and maintenance certificates under BS 5839, plus your fire risk assessment.
Can Icomply certificate emergency lighting as well?
Yes — related life-safety systems can be inspected and documented under our wider compliance services where scoped.
How quickly are certificates issued after work?
Usually at handover once testing is complete and any certifying defects are closed out.
fire-safety-certificate.seo_keywords: fire safety certificate, fire alarm certificate Stockport, fire safety paperwork North West, BS 5839 certificate Manchester, landlord fire certificate, Icomply

landlord-fire-alarm.intro: Landlord fire alarm duties cover detection and warning in HMOs, blocks of flats and many rented homes. Icomply installs and maintains landlord-focused fire alarm solutions across Stockport, Greater Manchester and nearby counties.
landlord-fire-alarm.body: We align system grade and category with the property type, your fire risk assessment and local expectations for rented stock. Agents appreciate single-supplier servicing across portfolios, with certificates filed per address. Tenant-safe working practices and clear appointment windows keep disruption down.
landlord-fire-alarm.meta_desc: Landlord fire alarms for HMOs, flats & rentals across Stockport & the North West. Install, service & certificates from Icomply.
focus_points: HMO and multi-let fire alarm design to suitable categories
Interlinked detection for houses and system solutions for blocks
Portfolio servicing with per-property certificates
Support for agents managing North West rental stock
faq: Do all rental properties need a full fire alarm panel?
Not always. Some houses need interlinked smoke and heat alarms; HMOs and larger buildings often need BS 5839 systems. We advise after reviewing the property.
Can you work directly with letting agents?
Yes. We provide consolidated invoicing options and certificate packs formatted for agency compliance files.
What about false alarms caused by tenants cooking?
Heat detectors in kitchens and well-sited multi-sensors reduce many cooking-related activations; we design with that in mind.
landlord-fire-alarm.seo_keywords: landlord fire alarm, HMO fire alarm Stockport, rental property fire alarm North West, landlord smoke alarms Manchester, block fire alarm, Icomply landlords

commercial-fire-alarm-system.intro: A commercial fire alarm system protects staff, customers and business continuity in offices, retail, hospitality and mixed-use premises. Icomply delivers end-to-end commercial systems from design through maintenance across the North West.
commercial-fire-alarm-system.body: Designs respect trading layouts, out-of-hours occupancy and interfaces with security or access control. We programme cause-and-effect so doors release, lifts home and plant shuts down in the right sequence. Fixed commercial quotes follow a site survey so finance teams can approve with confidence.
commercial-fire-alarm-system.meta_desc: Commercial fire alarm systems designed, installed & maintained across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West by Icomply.
focus_points: Commercial risk-based design for offices, retail and hospitality
Integration with access control, plant and monitoring
Addressable platforms for multi-floor and multi-tenant sites
Service contracts with audit-ready documentation
faq: Can systems serve multi-tenant commercial buildings?
Yes. We design zoning and panel access so landlords and tenants understand responsibilities, with landlord common-parts coverage clearly defined.
Do you offer 24/7 remote monitoring connections?
We can include signalling to alarm receiving centres where the risk assessment or insurer requires it.
Will installation disrupt trading?
Works are phased and often scheduled outside peak hours. Live detection strategies keep occupied areas protected during the project.
commercial-fire-alarm-system.seo_keywords: commercial fire alarm system, commercial fire alarm Stockport, office fire alarm North West, retail fire alarm Manchester, BS 5839 commercial, Icomply

industrial-fire-alarm.intro: Industrial fire alarm environments — factories, warehouses and logistics hubs — need rugged detection strategies that cope with dust, height and process heat. Icomply engineers industrial systems for North West sites with practical BS 5839 application.
industrial-fire-alarm.body: High-level detection, beam systems, aspirating options and carefully chosen heat devices feature where standard point detectors struggle. We coordinate isolations with production managers and document interfaces to suppression or process shutdown. Maintenance regimes reflect harsher conditions and higher fault risks.
industrial-fire-alarm.meta_desc: Industrial fire alarms for factories & warehouses across Stockport, Manchester & the North West. Robust BS 5839 design by Icomply.
focus_points: Detection strategies for high ceilings, dust and process areas
Beam, aspirating and specialist heat detection options
Interfaces to plant shutdown and industrial controls
Service plans suited to demanding industrial environments
faq: Why do warehouses often need beam detection?
High ceilings make point smoke detectors less effective or harder to maintain; beams cover large open volumes efficiently when designed correctly.
Can you work around continuous production?
Yes. We plan isolations, temporary cover and night works with site managers so output impact is minimised.
Do industrial sites still need manual call points?
Generally yes, positioned on escape routes to BS 5839 guidance, unless a documented variation is justified.
industrial-fire-alarm.seo_keywords: industrial fire alarm, warehouse fire alarm Stockport, factory fire detection North West, industrial BS 5839 Manchester, logistics fire alarm, Icomply industrial

fire-alarm-commissioning.intro: Fire alarm commissioning is the structured proving stage that turns a wired system into a certified, working life-safety installation. Icomply commissions new and upgraded systems across Stockport and the wider North West.
fire-alarm-commissioning.body: Engineers verify device addresses, zone integrity, sounder levels, battery autonomy and every agreed cause-and-effect action. Snags are closed before certificates are issued, not left as vague ‘client to monitor’ notes. Responsible persons receive a walkthrough so weekly tests start correctly from day one.
fire-alarm-commissioning.meta_desc: Fire alarm commissioning to BS 5839 across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. Full proving, certs & handover by Icomply.
focus_points: End-to-end functional proving of devices and outputs
Sound pressure and battery autonomy checks
Cause-and-effect verification against the agreed matrix
Certification, zone charts and user training at handover
faq: How long does commissioning take?
Small systems may take a few hours; large addressable networks can take days. Duration depends on device count and interface complexity.
Can you commission a system installed by another contractor?
We can, provided design information is available and installation quality allows safe proving. Major defects must be rectified first.
What documents are produced at commissioning?
Commissioning certificates, as-fitted details, cause-and-effect records, battery calculations where applicable, and a completed logbook section.
fire-alarm-commissioning.seo_keywords: fire alarm commissioning, BS 5839 commissioning Stockport, fire system handover North West, commission fire alarm Manchester, Icomply commissioning

fire-alarm-battery-replacement.intro: Fire alarm battery replacement restores standby power so panels and devices keep working during a mains failure. Icomply replaces sealed lead-acid and manufacturer-specified batteries on systems throughout the North West.
fire-alarm-battery-replacement.body: We size replacements to the original autonomy requirements, fit with correct polarity and fusing, then retest charge circuits. Ageing batteries are a leading cause of panel faults and failed inspections — proactive swaps during service visits prevent nuisance call-outs. Old batteries are disposed of responsibly.
fire-alarm-battery-replacement.meta_desc: Fire alarm battery replacement across Stockport, Manchester & the North West. Correct sizing, fit & retest by Icomply engineers.
focus_points: Standby batteries matched to panel load and autonomy
Safe replacement, polarity checks and charge verification
Proactive swaps during planned service visits
Responsible disposal of spent batteries
faq: How often should fire alarm batteries be replaced?
Typically every three to five years depending on type, environment and manufacturer guidance — sooner if testing shows reduced capacity.
Why is my panel showing a battery fault?
Common causes include end-of-life cells, poor connections or charger faults. We diagnose before simply fitting new batteries.
Do you replace batteries for wireless field devices too?
Yes, where your system uses radio devices with local cells; these follow manufacturer intervals separate from panel standby batteries.
fire-alarm-battery-replacement.seo_keywords: fire alarm battery replacement, fire panel batteries Stockport, SLA battery fire alarm North West, fire alarm battery fault Manchester, Icomply batteries

aspirating-smoke-detection.intro: Aspirating smoke detection (ASD) draws air through sampling pipes to spot microscopic smoke particles earlier than many point detectors. Icomply designs and maintains aspirating systems for high-value and high-ceiling North West sites.
aspirating-smoke-detection.body: Data halls, cold stores, heritage voids and warehouse roofs are typical applications where ASD excels. Pipe layout, hole sizing and sensitivity class are engineered — not improvised — so transport time and false signals stay controlled. Service includes filter checks, airflow verification and calibration support.
aspirating-smoke-detection.meta_desc: Aspirating smoke detection design, install & service across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. Early-warning ASD by Icomply.
focus_points: ASD design for warehouses, IT spaces and difficult voids
Sampling pipe networks engineered for balanced airflow
Sensitivity class selection to balance early warning and nuisance risk
Planned maintenance, filters and airflow proving
faq: Is aspirating detection better than standard smoke detectors?
It can detect much earlier in suitable environments, especially large volumes or clean spaces, but it costs more and needs proper design and upkeep.
Can ASD integrate with our existing fire panel?
Yes — aspirating units usually signal as zones or addressable inputs into the main fire alarm panel.
How often does ASD need servicing?
At least in line with BS 5839 and manufacturer guidance; dusty sites may need more frequent filter and pipe checks.
aspirating-smoke-detection.seo_keywords: aspirating smoke detection, ASD fire detection Stockport, VESDA style detection North West, warehouse aspirating system Manchester, Icomply ASD

beam-detection.intro: Beam detection protects wide open spaces by projecting an infrared beam across the ceiling void or high roof space. Icomply installs and aligns reflective and end-to-end beam detectors for North West warehouses, atria and sports halls.
beam-detection.body: Correct mounting height, separation and alignment are critical; we use manufacturer tools to set thresholds that catch real smoke without constant misalignment faults. Building movement and temperature swings are considered at design stage. Service visits re-check alignment and lens cleanliness so coverage stays reliable.
beam-detection.meta_desc: Beam smoke detection for warehouses & atria across Stockport, Manchester & the North West. Align, commission & service with Icomply.
focus_points: Reflective and transmitter/receiver beam detector options
Survey for mounting height, range and structural movement
Precision alignment and threshold commissioning
Maintenance to prevent dirt and drift-related faults
faq: When is beam detection recommended?
Large open floor plates with high ceilings where point detectors are impractical or insufficient often suit beam detection.
Will forklift exhaust set beams off?
Good design, correct sensitivity and sometimes multi-criteria strategies reduce nuisance alarms; we assess process risks on survey.
Can beams work with an addressable fire panel?
Yes. Beams are interfaced as monitored inputs or addressable modules depending on the chosen products.
beam-detection.seo_keywords: beam detection, beam smoke detector Stockport, warehouse beam detection North West, aspirating vs beam Manchester, fire beam install, Icomply

manual-call-point.intro: A manual call point lets occupants raise a fire alarm instantly on escape routes and exits. Icomply supplies, sites and replaces call points on conventional and addressable systems across the North West.
manual-call-point.body: We position MCP units at the correct heights and locations under BS 5839 guidance, with clear visibility and resettable elements where preferred. Weatherproof versions protect external exits, while transparent covers can reduce malicious activations in public areas. Each device is tested into the panel and logged.
manual-call-point.meta_desc: Manual call point supply, siting & replacement across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. BS 5839 MCP work by Icomply.
focus_points: Correct MCP siting on escape routes and storey exits
Resettable and break-glass options, including covers
Weatherproof call points for external doors
Addressable and conventional wiring or loop connection
faq: How high should a manual call point be mounted?
BS 5839 typically recommends a mounting height around 1.2 m to the operating element, subject to accessibility needs.
Can call point covers stop false alarms?
Hinged covers reduce accidental or malicious activations in many public buildings and are commonly specified.
Do you replace damaged call points quickly?
Yes — we stock common MCP types for popular systems and can often fit replacements on a short-notice visit.
manual-call-point.seo_keywords: manual call point, MCP fire alarm Stockport, break glass call point North West, resettable call point Manchester, fire alarm MCP install, Icomply

sounder-beacon.intro: Sounder beacons combine audible and visual warning so fire alarms reach people in noisy plant areas and those who cannot rely on sound alone. Icomply designs sounder-beacon coverage for inclusive, BS 5839-aware systems across the North West.
sounder-beacon.body: We calculate sound pressure levels for occupied spaces and place beacons where visual alarm devices support accessibility duties. Weatherproof and base-mounted options cover plant rooms, car parks and common parts. Commissioning includes measured checks, not assumptions that ‘it seems loud enough’.
sounder-beacon.meta_desc: Sounder beacon design & install for fire alarms across Stockport, Manchester & the North West. Audible-visual warning by Icomply.
focus_points: Combined sounder-beacon devices for dual-mode warning
Sound level design for plant, offices and common parts
Visual alarm devices supporting accessibility needs
Weatherproof units for external and harsh environments
faq: Are visual beacons a legal requirement?
Requirements depend on risk assessment and who occupies the building. Many workplaces and public buildings specify VADs for inclusive warning.
Why is my sounder too quiet in one room?
Doors, soft furnishings and distance reduce dB levels. We measure and add or reposition devices to meet design targets.
Can sounders be silenced while beacons stay on?
Some systems support differentiated control; we programme cause-and-effect to match your evacuation strategy.
sounder-beacon.seo_keywords: sounder beacon, fire alarm sounder Stockport, visual alarm device North West, VAD fire alarm Manchester, sounder beacon install, Icomply

voice-alarm-system.intro: A voice alarm system delivers spoken evacuation messages that can reduce confusion compared with tone-only sounders in complex buildings. Icomply installs and maintains voice alarm solutions for large North West premises that need phased or zoned evacuation.
voice-alarm-system.body: Shopping centres, transport hubs, education campuses and high-rises often benefit from clear, pre-recorded instructions. We engineer amplifier capacity, loudspeaker circuits and microphone facilities, then integrate with the fire detection cause-and-effect. Routine testing confirms message intelligibility remains acceptable as fit-outs change.
voice-alarm-system.meta_desc: Voice alarm systems for complex buildings across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. Design, install & maintain with Icomply.
focus_points: Zoned voice alarm design for phased evacuation strategies
Amplifier, loudspeaker and battery autonomy engineering
Integration with fire detection cause-and-effect
Intelligibility-focused commissioning and maintenance
faq: When is a voice alarm better than electronic sounders?
Large or complex buildings where occupants need instructions — not just a tone — often justify voice alarm under the fire strategy.
Can messages be customised for our site?
Yes. Approved message sets can cover full evacuate, alert and investigation stages depending on your strategy.
Do voice alarm systems need special maintenance?
They require competent servicing of amplification, batteries, loudspeaker circuits and message integrity in addition to detection maintenance.
voice-alarm-system.seo_keywords: voice alarm system, voice evacuation Stockport, VA system North West, phased evacuation alarm Manchester, fire voice alarm, Icomply

disabled-refuge-system.intro: A disabled refuge system provides two-way communication between refuge areas and a control point during evacuation. Icomply installs and services refuge systems for multi-storey buildings across Stockport and the North West.
disabled-refuge-system.body: We locate outstations in designated refuge spaces, ensure cabling is suitably protected, and prove speech quality at handover. Integration with fire strategies and management procedures is explained to the responsible person so equipment is not left unused. Maintenance visits check batteries, handsets and line integrity.
disabled-refuge-system.meta_desc: Disabled refuge systems installed & serviced across Stockport, Greater Manchester & the North West. Evacuation communication by Icomply.
focus_points: Refuge outstations sited to the building’s fire strategy
Two-way speech paths to the fire control or reception point
Fire-resisting cable practice and standby power checks
User guidance for responsible persons and marshals
faq: Is a disabled refuge system required in every building?

Multi-storey buildings with refuge areas in the fire strategy typically need a way for people to communicate until assisted evacuation is available. We confirm against your strategy and risk assessment.
Can refuge systems work during a power cut?
Yes — systems include standby batteries so communication continues when mains power fails.
Do you service existing refuge systems?
We maintain and repair common refuge brands, replace handsets and batteries, and retest speech paths on planned visits.
disabled-refuge-system.seo_keywords: disabled refuge system, refuge intercom Stockport, emergency voice communication North West, disabled refuge install Manchester, EVCS fire strategy, Icomply refuge
